Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. General elections were held in Antigua and Barbuda on 18 January 2023 to elect members of the House of Representatives. The Labour Party (ABLP) has held an absolute majority of 15 seats in the House of Representatives after the 2018 general election, with Gaston Browne remaining as prime minister. Browne initiated a constitutional referendum after the 2018 election, which was rejected by voters, and following the death of Elizabeth II in 2022, he announced his intention to organise a referendum for the transition of Antigua and Barbuda to a republican system. B. General elections were held on 18 January 2023 to elect members of the House of Representatives in Antigua and Barbuda. C. On January 18, 2023, general elections took place in Antigua and Barbuda with the purpose of selecting members for the House of Representatives. Following the 2018 general election, the Labour Party (ABLP) had maintained a clear majority, holding 15 seats in the House of Representatives, and Gaston Browne continued in his role as prime minister. After the 2018 election, Browne put forward a constitutional referendum, which was ultimately rejected by the electorate. Subsequently, in 2022, following the passing of Elizabeth II, he declared his plan to arrange a referendum concerning Antigua and Barbuda's transition to a republican form of government. In addition to the ABLP, several other political entities and individuals submitted their candidacies for the 2023 general election. These included the United Progressive Party (UPP), the Democratic National Alliance, the Barbuda People's Movement (BPM), and three independent political figures.
